content: Docker --rm container lifecycle: When docker run receives SIGTERM, it forwards the signal to the container (PID 1 inside container), waits for graceful exit, then removes the container. When docker run receives SIGKILL, it cannot forward the signal (SIGKILL cannot be caught or delegated), so the container continues running as a daemon-managed container and the --rm cleanup does NOT happen. This is a known Docker issue (moby/moby#46749). Implication: SIGTERM to docker run is required before SIGKILL to guarantee --rm container removal. A 2-second grace window between SIGTERM and SIGKILL gives docker time to stop the container.
